subject: App keeps crashing after the 4.2 update
incoming:
Hi team,

Since updating to version 4.2 yesterday, the mobile app crashes every time I open the Reports tab. I'm on an iPhone 13, iOS 17.5. I've already tried reinstalling the app and restarting my phone.

I need those reports for a client meeting on Thursday. Can you tell me if there's a workaround, and when a fix is expected?

Thanks,
Maya Prentiss
gold_reply:
Hi Maya,

I'm sorry about the crashes — that's frustrating right before a client meeting, and I appreciate you already trying a reinstall.

We've confirmed a bug in 4.2 affecting the Reports tab on iOS and a fix (4.2.1) is with Apple for review; we expect it live within 48 hours. In the meantime, your reports are fully available on the web dashboard at app.example.com/reports — the same data, exportable to PDF, so you'll be covered for Thursday.

I've added your ticket to the incident so you'll get an email the moment 4.2.1 ships. If the web dashboard gives you any trouble, reply here and I'll pull the exports for you myself.

Best,
Sam
Customer Support
candidate_reply:
Hi Maya,

I'm sorry about the crashes — that's frustrating right before a client meeting.

We've identified the bug in 4.2 and a fix is in progress. In the meantime, you can access your reports through our web dashboard at app.example.com/reports. The fix should be available within 48 hours.

I'll keep you updated on the progress.

Best,
Sam
Customer Support

subject: Charged twice this month
incoming:
Hi,

I was charged twice on the 3rd — two identical charges of $49 on my card ending 4417. I only have one subscription. Please refund the duplicate and confirm this won't happen again. Invoice numbers are INV-20931 and INV-20932.

Regards,
Henry Osei
gold_reply:
Hi Henry,

You're right, and I'm sorry — INV-20932 was a duplicate charge caused by a payment retry that fired after the first charge had already succeeded.

I've refunded the $49 to your card ending 4417 just now; it should appear within 3–5 business days depending on your bank. INV-20932 has been voided so your records stay clean, and I've credited one month of service to your account for the hassle.

On prevention: we've flagged the retry bug to our payments team and your account now has duplicate-charge protection enabled. If the refund hasn't landed by Friday, reply here and I'll chase it with the processor directly.

Apologies again,
Nadia
Billing Team
candidate_reply:
Hi Henry,

I've confirmed the duplicate charge and refunded $49 to your card ending 4417. The refund should appear in 3-5 business days. INV-20932 has been voided.

We've also flagged this to prevent future duplicates. Let me know if you don't see the refund by Friday.

Best,
Nadia
Billing Team

subject: Pricing for a team of 12
incoming:
Hi,

We're evaluating your platform for our design agency — 12 people. Could you send over pricing? Do you offer annual discounts?

Thanks,
Ruth Okafor
gold_reply:
Hi Ruth,

Thanks for reaching out — great to hear you're evaluating us for the agency!

For 12 seats you'd be on our Team plan at $18/user/month billed monthly, or $15/user/month billed annually (about a 17% saving — $2,160/year total). That includes unlimited projects, client review links, and priority support.

If it's helpful, I can set you up with a 14-day trial for the whole team plus a 30-minute walkthrough tailored to design-agency workflows. Would sometime this week suit for a quick call?

Best,
Marcus
Account Executive
candidate_reply:
Hi Ruth,

Thanks for your interest! For 12 seats, our Team plan is $18/user/month, or $15/user/month on annual billing — that's about 17% off. The plan includes unlimited projects and priority support.

Want me to set up a trial for your team? Happy to schedule a quick demo call too.

Best,
Marcus

subject: Q4 hiring plan
incoming:
Hi Alex,

I'm finalizing the Q4 hiring plan for leadership review. For your team, I have you down for 2 backend engineers and 1 designer. Can you confirm those numbers, flag any changes, and send me one-line justifications for each role by Wednesday EOD? If anything's changed since our last chat, let's grab 15 minutes.

Thanks,
Sofia Ramos
Head of People
gold_reply:
Hi Sofia,

Confirming with one change: 2 backend engineers stays, but I'd like to swap the designer for a product analyst — our design bandwidth is covered through Q1 after Kim's return, while analytics is now the bottleneck.

Justifications:
- Backend engineer #1: payments migration needs a dedicated owner; currently splitting one engineer across two workstreams.
- Backend engineer #2: on-call load is unsustainable at 4 engineers; fifth brings rotation to industry standard.
- Product analyst (replacing designer): three roadmap decisions slipped last quarter waiting on data; no dedicated analyst on the team today.

Happy to grab 15 minutes if the swap complicates the plan — I'm free Wednesday before noon.

Thanks,
Alex

Hi Sofia,

Confirmed: 2 backend engineers and 1 designer works. But I'd actually like to swap the designer for a product analyst — here's why:

- Backend #1: Payments migration needs a dedicated owner
- Backend #2: On-call rotation is unsustainable at 4 people
- Product analyst (swap): Analytics is our bottleneck; 3 roadmap decisions slipped last quarter

Happy to chat if the swap needs discussion. Free Wednesday morning.

Best,
Alex

subject: Third time reporting sync bug
incoming:
Hi,

This is the third ticket I've opened about calendar sync silently dropping events. Each time I've been told it's fixed, and each time it breaks again within a week. I've now missed two customer calls because of this.

Honestly, I'm close to moving to a competitor. Before I do: can you escalate this properly, tell me what's actually causing it, and give me a straight answer on whether it will be fixed for good?

Dan Whitfield, Ops Lead, Corvid Logistics
gold_reply:
Hi Dan,

You're right to be frustrated, and I'm sorry — three tickets for the same issue is not acceptable.
candidate_reply:
Hi Dan,

I completely understand your frustration — three tickets for the same issue is unacceptable, and missing customer calls has real consequences.

Here's the straight answer: the root cause is a race condition in two-way sync when events are edited simultaneously. Previous fixes were patches, not permanent solutions. The actual fix is in code review and ships Tuesday.

I've escalated your case to our sync engineer Lena (cc'd), enabled verbose logging on your account, and will personally follow up after Tuesday's release.

If it doesn't hold, I'll arrange a call with engineering leadership. You have my word this stays open until you confirm it's resolved.

Regards,
Sam
Customer Support, Escalations

subject: Disputing overage charges
incoming:
To whom it may concern,

Our latest invoice includes $3,840 in seat overage charges. Our contract (signed March, ref C-2214) specifies a true-up at renewal, not monthly overage billing. We won't be paying these charges.

Victoria Ash
CFO, Meridian Partners
gold_reply:
Dear Victoria,

Thank you for flagging this — you're right. Section 4.2 of C-2214 specifies annual true-up at renewal. The monthly billing was applied in error during a system migration.
candidate_reply:
Dear Victoria,

Thank you for bringing this to our attention. You are correct — Section 4.2 of contract C-2214 specifies annual true-up at renewal. The monthly overage billing was applied in error during our May system migration.

I've removed the $3,840 in overage charges and attached a corrected invoice. The additional seats will be reconciled at your March renewal per the contract terms.

We're also auditing other migrated accounts to prevent recurrence. I've noted the true-up arrangement on your account.

Kind regards,
Nadia Kowalski
Billing Team Lead

subject: Security compliance questions
incoming:
Hello,

We're close to a decision but our security team has three questions: Are you SOC 2 Type II certified? Where is customer data hosted? And do you support SSO via Okta?

David Lindqvist
IT Director
gold_reply:
Hi David,

1. SOC 2 Type II: Yes, certified since 2023.
2. Data hosting: AWS us-east-1 by default, EU option available.
3. Okta SSO: Yes, SAML 2.0 supported.

Happy to answer your security questions:

3. Okta SSO: Fully supported via SAML 2.0, with SCIM provisioning on Business plan.

I'll send the SOC 2 report and security whitepaper once we have a mutual NDA in place — I can have that over within the hour.

Anything else your security team needs?

Best regards,
Marcus
Account Executive

subject: Expense policy concerns
incoming:
Hi Alex,

The new expense policy is causing real friction. The $25 meal cap doesn't work in SF or NYC, the 30-day reimbursement window penalizes people who travel back-to-back, and requiring VP pre-approval for anything over $200 is slowing client visits.

People are quietly eating costs. Can you raise this with leadership?

Jordan
gold_reply:
Hi Jordan,

Thank you for raising this — people absorbing work costs is not acceptable. I'll take it to leadership on Thursday.
candidate_reply:
Hi Jordan,

Thank you for raising this directly — people quietly absorbing work costs is exactly what I need to know about.

Yes, I'll take all three issues to leadership at Thursday's ops meeting. Can you gather 4-5 specific examples (city, actual cost vs cap, delayed reimbursements with dates) by Wednesday? I'll present them anonymized.

My recommendations will be: city-tier meal caps, per-trip reimbursement windows, and raising the pre-approval threshold to $500 with manager sign-off instead of VP.

In the meantime, tell anyone who's out of pocket to file anyway and flag me — I'll approve overages as policy exceptions.

Thanks for being a good rep on this,
Alex
